title: Work-loop gate diet: green receipts + docs-only gate tiering + event-driven waits

tracker.mergeBaseFlow:
# Work-loop gate diet: green receipts + docs-only gate tiering + event-driven waits

## Goal & Context

The fn-89 pipeline run was trace-mined end to end (2026-07-19 post-mortem): of 2h41 work-tick-to-PR, actual implementation was ~425s (4.4%) while ~50% went to the full unittest suite executing 10 times in one work stage. Every worker runs the suite twice (pre-baseline at `agents/worker.md` "Baseline check" + post-gate "Verify before completing"), each pre-baseline re-proves green on exactly the commit the previous worker's post-gate certified, and the host re-runs tests again at `skills/flow-next-work/phases.md` Phase 4. Worst case: the docs-only task spent 84% of its 55 minutes on suite x4 + smoke x2 - gates its diff could not break - while the REVIEW layer's deterministic triage-skip classified the same diff in 2 seconds.

The fix is mechanical, not semantic: (a) remember, keyed by exact commit hash, that a tree state was already proven green; (b) classify a diff by file path as unable to affect the executable surface. Both are pure plumbing - the flowctl carve-out. This spec deliberately stays outside fn-83 territory: "did this change semantically affect that plan?" remains a judgment question and its deterministic-skip ban stands (decision record `plan-sync-skip-gate-not-viable-2026-07-03`, module phases.md, binds). "Is this byte-identical tree already proven green?" and "does this diff touch only non-executable paths?" involve no judgment.

**Two whitelists, two purposes (design tension resolved):** the review triage-skip (`TRIAGE_ARTIFACT_PREFIXES`) forces REVIEW on `.flow/specs|tasks|epics` because review protects MEANING; the gate tier treats most `.flow/**` as gate-safe because gates protect EXECUTABLES - a spec md cannot break the Python suite. Both layers are correct independently; the spec states this in prose, the evidence strings name their layer, and the gate classifier REUSES the triage helpers (`TRIAGE_DOC_EXTS`, path normalization) so the two lists share primitives rather than drifting.

## Acceptance criteria

- **R1:** Green receipts - baseline reuse, command-fingerprinted. When a full gate passes at a worker post-gate OR host Phase 4, the site records it: `flowctl gate receipt --gate <id> --command "<exact command string>"` writes ONE FILE PER RECEIPT at `.flow/tmp/green-receipts/<sha8>-<gate_id>.json` = `{schema: 1, head_sha, gate_id, command_sha256, timestamp}` via `atomic_write_json` (per-receipt files: no shared ledger, no lock, no read-modify-write race). `gate_id` is a bounded slug validated at BOTH the `receipt` and `check` boundaries (`^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9._-]{0,63}$`, with the literals `.` and `..` explicitly rejected - it is interpolated into the receipt filename, so traversal characters like `/`, `\`, `..` must be impossible; invalid ids exit 2); the CONTRACT is `command_sha256` (sha256 of the exact command string) - a receipt certifies exactly one command. The honor probe `flowctl gate check --gate <id> --command "<cmd>"` exits 0 iff ALL hold: receipt `head_sha` == current full HEAD; `command_sha256` matches the probe's command; worktree cleanliness - `git status --porcelain=v1 -z --no-renames --untracked-files=all` reports NO entries outside the ignore set, where the ignore set is `.flow/**` MINUS `.flow/bin/**` MINUS `.flow/config.json` (dirty `.flow/bin/**` or `.flow/config.json` is execution-affecting and forces exit 1; the receipts dir under `.flow/tmp/` never self-dirties); `0 <= age <= 24h` (lower bound rejects future timestamps/clock skew; TTL bounds toolchain drift); `schema == 1`. ANY other condition (missing, unparseable, mismatch, dirty, stale, future, no repo) -> exit 1, run the full gate as today; real errors exit 2+. The worker maps its Quick commands to (gate_id, command) pairs verbatim - only an exact command match honors. Lint/format stay always-run (fast, not receipted, v1). Remote CI gates (land's tri-state, GH Actions) are explicitly OUT of scope - a green receipt never implies CI can be skipped.
- **R2:** Docs-only gate tiering with ORDERED precedence. `flowctl gate classify --base <ref>` classifies the CUMULATIVE task diff at EACH gate decision point (never locked at task start - a fix-loop commit that adds code reclassifies to full on the next gate). Path collection is plumbing-safe: `git diff --name-only -z --no-renames <ref>...HEAD` UNION `git status --porcelain=v1 -z --no-renames --untracked-files=all` paths (NUL-delimited, renames uncollapsed so both sides count, untracked enumerated per-file; handles spaces/newlines/backslashes). Precedence, first match wins per path: (1) FORCE-FULL - any code/config extension anywhere (`TRIAGE_CODE_EXTS` plus `.py .sh .cmd .ps1 .toml .json .yaml .yml` - extension beats prefix, so a `.py` under `docs/` is FULL), `scripts/**`, `plugins/flow-next/scripts/**`, `plugins/flow-next/tests/**`, `.flow/bin/**`, `.flow/config.json`, `plugins/flow-next/{skills,agents,commands,references,templates,hooks}/**`, `plugins/flow-next/codex/**` - these can break the suite (prose-contract tests pin skill/agent wording) or ARE executable/mirror surface; (2) SAFE - `docs/**`, `agent_docs/**`, `optimization/**`, root `CHANGELOG.md`/`README*`/`GLOSSARY.md`/`STRATEGY.md`, `plugins/flow-next/docs/**` (`.md`/`.mdx`/`.txt` only), remaining `.flow/**`; (3) anything unmatched -> FULL. Exit 0 (tier-B) iff the diff is non-empty and every path is SAFE; empty diff or any FORCE-FULL/unmatched path -> exit 1; errors 2+ (fail-closed to full gates). Tier-B gate set = lint/format where repo-configured, NOTHING else - mirror-source paths are never tier-B (no conditional mirror branch; sync-codex regen stays part of the normal full-gate workflow for prose changes exactly as today). Classifier REUSES `_classify_triage_path`'s normalization helper and the `TRIAGE_DOC_EXTS`/`TRIAGE_CODE_EXTS` constants - no reimplementation. `--json` emits per-path class + reason for evidence lines.
- **R3:** Loud skips, machine-parseable, per-layer sinks. WORKER skips (pre-baseline receipt honor, Verify-site tier-B) write evidence lines into the task's `tests[]` (fn-99 schema compatible - plain strings): `GATE_SKIPPED:<gate_id>:<green-receipt <sha8>|docs-only>` plus a human clause, echoed in the worker summary. HOST Phase 4 skips cannot land in task evidence (tasks are already done) - they go to the Phase 5 final summary block as `Gates: baseline reused (green receipt <sha8>)` / `Gates: docs-only tier-B` lines. Never a silent skip at either layer. Review-triage receipts stay `mode: triage_skip` - the layers remain distinguishable.
- **R4:** Event-driven waits. Fixed full-interval sleeps in the work-loop prose are replaced by short-interval bounded until-loops: `references/codex-delegation.md:357-364` (seq 1 6; sleep 10 -> poll every 2s, same 60s bound) and any fixed sleeps in `agents/worker.md`/`phases.md`. Watchdog patterns follow the group-kill lesson (memory `bash-deadline-watchdogs-orphaned-sleep-2026-07-16`).
- **R5:** Scope guard. NO semantic skipping anywhere: predicates are commit-hash equality, worktree cleanliness, receipt age, and path membership ONLY. The fn-83 decision record binds; its record gains a one-line forward-pointer addendum ("fn-102's hash/path-only gate diet is outside this ban - see spec Decision Context"). A regression test asserts the classifier forces FULL gates for every skill/agent/command `.md`, `.flow/bin/**`, and any code extension.
- **R6:** Pre-land end-to-end proof (no cyclic acceptance). BEFORE landing, in a scratch worktree on the implementation branch, exercise the WIRED prose paths end to end: (a) docs-only probe - a scratch docs diff driven through the Verify-site classify decision produces tier-B with GATE_SKIPPED evidence lines and ZERO full-suite executions (instrumented by counting suite invocations); (b) receipt probe - full suite run once, receipt written via the wired command, `gate check` with the same command exits 0, then a dirtied code file / changed command string / future timestamp each flip it to exit 1 (fail-closed demonstrated live); (c) full suite green at branch tip (zero green->red regressions). All recorded in task evidence with counts. The post-land trace post-mortem (target: gate share <= 50% of the fn-89 baseline) is a tracked FOLLOW-UP measurement, not an acceptance condition of this spec.
- **R7:** Cross-platform. flowctl changes land in `plugins/flow-next/scripts/flowctl.py` AND the byte-identical `.flow/bin/flowctl.py` copy (dual-copy invariant). Worker/phases prose edits walk the CLAUDE.md cross-platform checklist: sync-codex.sh x2 idempotent + guards green; canonical prose stays host-portable (no Claude-only constructs; flowctl invocations use the `$FLOWCTL` fallback chain). Classifier is pathlib/normalized (Windows-safe). Worktree-mode workers: `.flow/tmp` is per-checkout, so receipts are naturally scoped per worktree - no cross-worktree reuse (documented, correct: different worktrees have different HEADs).

## Approach

Mirror the review triage-skip architecture one layer down. New flowctl subcommand group `gate` (argparse `add_subparsers` pattern like `memory`/`prospect`): `gate receipt --gate <id> --command <cmd>` (write, called after a passing full gate), `gate check --gate <id> --command <cmd>` (honor probe, exit-coded), `gate classify --base <ref>` (tier decision, exit-coded). Receipts are per-file (no shared ledger). Worker prose wires three sites: Baseline check (worker.md:52-63) consults `gate check` per (gate_id, command) before running; Verify-before-completing (worker.md:364-371) runs classify then full-or-tier-B gates and writes receipts for passing full gates; evidence write (worker.md:383-390) carries the GATE_SKIPPED lines. Host phases.md Phase 4 (475-483): the host captures a SPEC-RUN BASE ref once before the task loop (Phase 2 branch point - the branch's merge-base with the base branch, persisted to `.flow/tmp/spec_base` alongside the existing `base_commit` pattern) and uses it for its own classify calls; host skip outcomes surface in the Phase 5 summary (R3). Opt-out: none needed - fail-closed semantics make the feature invisible when receipts/classification don't apply.

## Key files / interfaces

- `plugins/flow-next/scripts/flowctl.py` + `.flow/bin/flowctl.py` (dual-copy): `gate` subcommand group near triage-skip (constants ~26652, cmd registration ~32437); `atomic_write_json` (2034)
- `plugins/flow-next/agents/worker.md` (Baseline check 52-63, BASE_COMMIT pattern 65-72, post-gate 364-371, evidence 383-390)
- `plugins/flow-next/skills/flow-next-work/phases.md` (Phase 4 Quality 475-483)
- `plugins/flow-next/skills/flow-next-work/references/codex-delegation.md` (357-364 poll loop)
- `scripts/sync-codex.sh` (mirror regen for prose changes)
- `plugins/flow-next/tests/test_gate_receipt.py`, `test_gate_classify.py` (new; hermetic unittest per test_qa_receipt.py conventions)
- `plugins/flow-next/docs/flowctl.md` (new `### gate` section on the triage-skip template), `GLOSSARY.md` (Green receipt term, relates_to Triage skip), `CHANGELOG.md` (Unreleased)
- `.flow/memory/knowledge/decisions/plan-sync-skip-gate-not-viable-2026-07-03.md` (forward-pointer addendum)

## Decision Context

- Post-mortem evidence: fn-89 run, suite x10 = ~4,800s of 9,640s; docs-only task 84% gate time; implementation 4.4%. (memory `session-2026-07-19-fn89-shipped`)
- Per-receipt files over a JSON ledger: eliminates the concurrent read-modify-write lost-update the gap analysis flagged; atomic file create is the only write primitive needed.
- `.flow/bin/**` carve-out: the deployed CLI copy is executable surface - a gate-diet patch must never classify its own live code as docs.
- 24h TTL: hash equality cannot see toolchain/dependency drift on an unchanged tree; a day-scoped receipt bounds that exposure cheaply. No other TTL/pruning in v1 (receipts are tiny; `.flow/tmp` is ephemeral).
- Classification at each gate decision over the cumulative live diff, never locked at task start: a docs-then-code fix-loop must escalate to full gates automatically.
- Review-triage vs gate-tier whitelists: same primitives, different purposes (meaning vs executables) - stated in prose, shared helpers, layer-named evidence.
- fn-83 contrast: that gate was a deterministic proxy for a semantic question and produced a false skip; every predicate here is hash/path/age membership. fn-101's burden-of-proof framing: these belong IN flowctl because no judgment is involved.
- fn-85 sequences AFTER this spec (its scenario matrices baseline the same worker/phases prose); fn-94 touches the same codex-delegation paragraph - coordinate wording at fn-94 plan time.
- Review triage-skip precedent anchors: whitelist constants flowctl.py:26652-26748, `_classify_triage_path` ~26773 (normalization), `_triage_deterministic` 26870-26974 (fail-closed), `cmd_triage_skip` 27125-27310 (receipt shape + exit codes).
